user_query:
I need a small, runnable validation suite to quality-check our press assets before we pitch journalists. Please implement and run a script or command-line program that reads the inputs under input/ and produces three artifacts under outputs/ as described below. Do not use the Internet; work offline with the provided files.

Inputs (already in the workspace):
- input/media_contacts.csv
- input/campaign_focus.json
- input/campaign_key_messages.txt
- input/press_release.html

Required validations and cross-checks:
1) media_contacts.csv
   - Confirm required headers exist: Outlet, Name, Email, City, State, Beats.
   - Validate emails using a basic format check (contains '@' and a domain part).
   - Detect and report duplicate contacts by Email.
   - For each contact, verify at least one Beats entry (semicolon-separated) intersects with focus_topics from input/campaign_focus.json (case-insensitive).
2) press_release.html
   - Extract title, meta description, canonical link (href), and the <time datetime="..."> value.
   - Verify the <time datetime> equals launch_date in input/campaign_focus.json.
   - Verify the canonical link's domain matches organization_domain in input/campaign_focus.json.
   - Check that at least three distinct lines from input/campaign_key_messages.txt appear in the article content.
   - Verify there is a press contact email in a mailto: link and that it appears exactly once in media_contacts.csv.

Outputs (must be created by running your script):
- outputs/validation_report.json: A machine-readable summary with fields:
  - tests: an array of objects, each with {name, status, details}. Use these test names exactly:
    - contacts_required_columns
    - contacts_valid_emails
    - contacts_duplicates_detected
    - contacts_focus_topics_alignment
    - press_meta_extracted
    - press_launch_date_matches
    - press_canonical_matches_org_domain
    - press_key_messages_coverage
    - press_contact_email_exists_in_contacts
  - summary: {passed: <int>, failed: <int>}
  Each test's status must be either "pass" or "fail", and details should briefly explain counts or findings.
- outputs/clean_contacts.csv: A deduplicated CSV of contacts who passed both the email format check and the focus_topics intersection check. Keep the original columns (Outlet, Name, Email, City, State, Beats) and ensure Beats are preserved as semicolon-separated.
- outputs/extracted_press_meta.json: The structured extraction from press_release.html, including at least {title, description, canonical, date, matched_messages_count, matched_messages, domain_matches}.

Please ensure the suite can be re-run to reproduce the same outputs from the provided inputs and that all paths match exactly as specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
